#include <hexi/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/stream_base.h>
#include <hexi/ostream.h>
#include <hexi/concepts.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << hexi::length_adaptor<uint32_t>(username);
        stream << scores.size();
        stream << scores;
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(metadata);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor buffer;
    hexi::ostream stream(buffer);
    packet.serialise(stream);
    return buffer.get_data();
}
```</answer>

    // Skip the username data
    hexi::skip(data, username_len);
    
    // Read scores count and skip the data
    uint32_t score_count = hexi::read<uint32_t>(data);
    hexi::skip(data, score_count * sizeof(uint32_t));
    
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct UserData {
    uint64_t user_id;
    uint8_t age;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream(user_id, age, name);
    }
};

UserData deserialize_user_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream_reader reader(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    UserData user;
    user.serialise(reader);
    return user;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <string_view>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint8_t version;
    uint8_t flags;
    uint16_t packet_id;
};

PacketHeader read_packet_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    return *reinterpret_cast<const PacketHeader*>(buffer.data());
}

std::string_view read_message_until_delimiter(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    auto null_pos = hexi::algorithm::find_first_of(buffer, 0);
    if (null_pos != buffer.npos) {
        return std::string_view(reinterpret_cast<const char*>(buffer.data()), null_pos);
    } else {
        return std::string_view();
    }
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    DataResult result;
    
    // Check minimum buffer size for header
    if (buffer.size() < 5) {
        result.data_type = 0;
        return result;
    }
    
    // Preview data_type without advancing read pointer
    result.data_type = buffer[0];
    
    // Read item_count from next 4 bytes
    hexi::buffer_adaptor header_adaptor(std::span<const uint8_t>(buffer.data() + 1, 4));
    hexi::binary_stream header_stream(header_adaptor);
    uint32_t item_count;
    header_stream >> item_count;
    
    // Calculate required buffer size
    size_t required_size = 5 + item_count * 4;
    if (buffer.size() < required_size) {
        result.data_type = 0;
        return result;
    }
    
    // Read items based on data_type
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        // Integers
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        hexi::buffer_adaptor items_adaptor(std::span<const uint8_t>(buffer.data() + 5, item_count * 4));
        hexi::binary_stream items_stream(items_adaptor);
        for (auto& item : result.int_items) {
            items_stream >> item;
        }
    } else {
        // Floats
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        hexi::buffer_adaptor items_adaptor(std::span<const uint8_t>(buffer.data() + 5, item_count * 4));
        hexi::binary_stream items_stream(items_adaptor);
        for (auto& item : result.float_items) {
            items_stream >> item;
        }
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

But since we checked available() before, which is capacity - size, the required_space must be <= available(), so capacity >= size + required_space. Therefore, after advancing, the new size is size + required_space, which is <= capacity. So no reallocation is needed. Therefore, the write_ptr remains valid.

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    // Calculate required space
    size_t required_space = sizeof(uint32_t) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t);
    
    // Create buffer adaptor
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer.data(), buffer.size(), buffer.capacity());
    
    // Check available space
    if (adaptor.available() < required_space) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Get write pointer
    uint8_t* write_ptr = adaptor.write_ptr();
    
    // Create binary stream
    hexi::binary_stream stream(write_ptr, required_space);
    
    // Write header
    stream.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    // Write items using write_container
    hexi::write_container(stream, items);
    
    // Advance write pointer
    adaptor.advance_write_ptr(required_space);
    
    return true;
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;
};

PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    
    // Check if there's enough data for the header (8 bytes)
    if (!adaptor.check_available(8)) {
        return PacketHeader{}; // Return default header if insufficient data
    }
    
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    PacketHeader header;
    
    // Read the header fields using zero-copy operations
    header.magic = stream.read_uint32();
    header.version = stream.read_uint16();
    header.flags = stream.read_uint16();
    
    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/dynamic_tls_buffer.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>

}

size_t get_file_readable_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    for (uint8_t value : data) {
        buffer.push_back(static_cast<std::byte>(value));
    }
}

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> read_data_from_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    size_t count = 0;
    while (!buffer.empty() && count < bytes_to_read) {
        std::byte value = buffer.front();
        result.push_back(static_cast<uint8_t>(value));
        buffer.pop_front();
        count++;
    }
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
struct BufferInfo {
    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo<> get_buffer_info(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, 1024>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo<> info;
    info.capacity = buffer.capacity();
    info.current_size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = info.capacity - info.current_size;
    return info;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer.read(i) == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read::npos;
}

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer.read(i) == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer.read(i) == value) {
            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    std::size_t bytes_written = 0;
    while (value > 0x7F) {
        uint8_t byte = static_cast<uint8_t>(value & 0x7F) | 0x80;
        writer.write(&byte, 1);
        value >>= 7;
        bytes_written++;
    }
    uint8_t byte = static_cast<uint8_t>(value & 0x7F);
    writer.write(&byte, 1);
    return ++bytes_written;
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    T result = 0;
    int shift = 0;
    uint8_t byte;
    
    do {
        reader.read(&byte, 1);
        result |= static_cast<T>(byte & 0x7F) << shift;
        shift += 7;
    } while (byte & 0x80);
    
    return result;
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(adaptor);
    
    std::size_t bytes_written = encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    
    adaptor.reset();
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(adaptor);
    
    T decoded_value = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    
    return decoded_value == original_value;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string result;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(result);
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstddef>

using namespace hexi::detail;

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {
    const char* src_start = static_cast<const char*>(src);
    const char* src_end = src_start + src_len;
    const char* dst_start = static_cast<const char*>(dst);
    const char* dst_end = dst_start + dst_len;
    
    return (src_start < dst_end) && (dst_start < src_end);
}

bool safe_memory_copy(void* destination, const void* source, std::size_t length) {
    if (check_regions_overlap(source, length, destination, length)) {
        memmove(destination, source, length);
    } else {
        memcpy(destination, source, length);
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UnderrunInfo {
    std::size_t read_size;
    std::size_t total_read;
    std::size_t buff_size;
    bool occurred;
};

UnderrunInfo catch_buffer_underrun() {
    UnderrunInfo info{0, 0, 0, false};
    try {
        // Simulate throwing a buffer_underrun exception with test values
        throw hexi::exception("buffer_underrun", 
            "read_size=5,total_read=10,buff_size=15");
    } catch (const hexi::exception& ex) {
        // Assuming the exception provides getters for the values
        // In a real scenario, the buffer_underrun exception would have specific getters
        // For this example, we'll parse the message string
        std::string msg = ex.what();
        info.read_size = std::stoul(msg.substr(9, 2)); // Extract "5" from "read_size=5"
        info.total_read = std::stoul(msg.substr(16, 2)); // Extract "10" from "total_read=10"
        info.buff_size = std::stoul(msg.substr(24, 2)); // Extract "15" from "buff_size=15"
        info.occurred = true;
    }
    return info;
}

UnderrunInfo safe_read_with_underrun_check(
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, 
    void* destination, 
    std::size_t length) {
    UnderrunInfo info{0, 0, 0, false};
    try {
        // Attempt to read data from the buffer
        buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(destination), length);
    } catch (const hexi::exception& ex) {
        if (ex.category() == "buffer_underrun") {
            // Extract information from the exception
            // Assuming the exception provides getters for the values
            // In a real implementation, the buffer_underrun exception would have specific getters
            std::string msg = ex.what();
            info.read_size = std::stoul(msg.substr(9, 2));
            info.total_read = std::stoul(msg.substr(16, 2));
            info.buff_size = std::stoul(msg.substr(24, 2));
            info.occurred = true;
        }
    }
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct ReadLimitInfo {
    std::size_t read_size;
    std::size_t total_read;
    std::size_t read_limit;
    bool occurred;
};

ReadLimitInfo catch_stream_read_limit() {
    ReadLimitInfo info{0, 0, 0, false};
    try {
        // Simulate throwing a stream_read_limit exception
        throw hexi::exception::stream_read_limit(512, 1024, 2048);
    } catch (const hexi::exception::stream_read_limit& e) {
        info.read_size = e.get_read_size();
        info.total_read = e.get_total_read();
        info.read_limit = e.get_read_limit();
        info.occurred = true;
    }
    return info;
}

ReadLimitInfo safe_read_with_limit_check(hexi::pmc::binary_stream& stream, void* destination, std::size_t length) {
    ReadLimitInfo info{0, 0, 0, false};
    try {
        stream.read(destination, length);
    } catch (const hexi::exception::stream_read_limit& e) {
        info.read_size = e.get_read_size();
        info.total_read = e.get_total_read();
        info.read_limit = e.get_read_limit();
        info.occurred = true;
    }
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstddef>

struct ExceptionDetails {
    std::size_t read_size;
    std::size_t total_read;
    std::size_t limit_or_size;
    std::string message;
};

ExceptionDetails extract_underrun_info(const hexi::buffer_underrun& e) {
    ExceptionDetails details;
    details.read_size = e.read_size();
    details.total_read = e.total_read();
    details.limit_or_size = e.buff_size();
    details.message = e.message();
    return details;
}

ExceptionDetails extract_read_limit_info(const hexi::stream_read_limit& e) {
    ExceptionDetails details;
    details.read_size = e.read_size();
    details.total_read = e.total_read();
    details.limit_or_size = e.read_limit();
    details.message = e.message();
    return details;
}

std::string get_exception_message(const hexi::exception& e) {
    return e.what();
}
```</answer>

So the adaptor for the elements would be hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()).

So the code for serializing the tags would be:

hexi::serialize(buffer, tags, hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));

Similarly for the data string:

hexi::serialize(buffer, data, hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));

But wait, the data is a string, not a vector. So when using prefixed_varint on a string, it would first write the string's length as varint, then the raw bytes. That's correct.

So the serialize function would start with an empty vector<uint8_t>, then serialize each component in order.

Putting it all together:

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id as varint
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.packet_id, hexi::varint());
    
    // Serialize tags using prefixed_varint for the vector of strings
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.tags, hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));
    
    // Serialize data using prefixed_varint for the string
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.data, hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));
    
    return buffer;
}

For deserialization, we need to read from the buffer in reverse order. The deserialization functions would read the packet_id first, then the tags, then the data.

The deserialization would use hexi::deserialize with the same adaptors.

So the deserialization function:

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    auto it = buffer.begin();
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    packet.packet_id = hexi::deserialize<uint32_t>(it, buffer.end(), hexi::varint());
    
    // Deserialize tags
    packet.tags = hexi::deserialize<std::vector<std::string>>(it, buffer.end(), 
        hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));
    
    // Deserialize data
    packet.data = hexi::deserialize<std::string>(it, buffer.end(), 
        hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));
    
    return packet;
}

Wait, but the deserialization of the tags vector would first read the size of the vector as a varint, then for each element, read the string's length as varint and then the characters. So the adaptor for the vector's elements is the prefixed_varint of varint and raw. That's correct.

But I need to make sure that the iterators are correctly passed. The hexi::deserialize function advances the iterator, so the order is important.

Then the data is serialized similarly.

When deserializing, we start at the beginning of the buffer. First, read the packet_id. Then read the tags vector: first read the size as varint, then for each tag, read the length as varint and then the characters. Then read the data string: read its length as varint and then the characters.

struct CompactPacket {

    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id using varint encoding
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.packet_id, hexi::varint());
    
    // Serialize tags using prefixed_varint (size + each tag's length + content)
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.tags, hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));
    
    // Serialize data using prefixed_varint (length + content)
    hexi::serialize(buffer, packet.data, hexi::prefixed_varint(hexi::varint(), hexi::raw()));
    
    return buffer;
}

    // Deserialize data string

    return packet;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator allocator;
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::stream stream(allocator, adaptor);

    // Write header

    // Write data length as uint32_t

    stream.write(data_length);
    
    // Write data string

    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00)
    stream.fill<16>(0x00);
    
    // Write footer
    stream.write(packet.footer);

    return buffer;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator allocator;
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::stream stream(allocator, adaptor);

    // Write header
    stream.write(packet.header);
    
    // Write data length as uint32_t
    uint32_t data_length = static_cast<uint32_t>(packet.data.size());
    stream.write(data_length);
    
    // Write data string
    stream.write(packet.data.data(), packet.data.size());
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00)
    stream.fill<16>(0x00);
    
    // Write footer
    stream.write(packet.footer);

    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    // Default constructor required by block_allocator
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct the first object
    auto* obj1 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj1) PoolObject(1, 1.5);
    objects.push_back(obj1);
    
    // Allocate and construct the second object
    auto* obj2 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj2) PoolObject(2, 2.5);
    objects.push_back(obj2);
    
    // Allocate and construct the third object
    auto* obj3 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj3) PoolObject(3, 3.5);
    objects.push_back(obj3);
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_read_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& input_data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor writer(buffer);
    
    // Check available space before writing (though buffer may resize)
    std::size_t free_space = writer.free();
    if (input_data.size() > free_space) {
        // Resize buffer if necessary
        buffer.resize(input_data.size());
    }
    
    writer.write(input_data.data(), input_data.size());
    
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor reader(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(reader.size());
    reader.read(result.data(), result.size());
    
    return result;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor reader(buffer_data);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(peek_size);
    
    // Use read_ptr() to get current read position
    const uint8_t* read_ptr = reader.read_ptr();
    // Copy data without advancing the read pointer
    std::memcpy(result.data(), read_ptr, peek_size);
    // Consume the data to advance the read pointer for subsequent reads
    reader.read(result.data(), peek_size);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    TypedData data;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}

};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor buffer;
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);

    uint32_t be_message_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);

    stream.write(be_checksum);

}

        adaptor.write(converted_pressure);
    }
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor.read(sensor_id);
        uint32_t count;
        adaptor.read(count);
        temperatures.resize(count);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
            float temp;
            adaptor.read(temp);
            hexi::little_to_native_inplace(temp);
            temperatures[i] = temp;
        }
        double pressure;
        adaptor.read(pressure);
        hexi::little_to_native_inplace(pressure);
        this->pressure = pressure;
    }
};
